serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of grilled pork skewers in Singapore is 3 sticks (approximately 90g), as described in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish.

nutrient_source.title: Grilled Pork (烤猪肉串) — Three Strings (3串)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical entry for 烤猪肉串 Grilled Pork (Three Strings, 3串), cr_id G00010920. All per_100g values preserved from the HPB source with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — Grilled Pork Skewers contain negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g per HPB Singapore Foodo)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for this food as carbohydrate content is 0g/100g per HPB Singapore Foodo. GI is physiologically meaningless for foods with negligible carbohydrates. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign GI values to foods with negligible carbohydrate content; this approach is consistent with their published methodology. gi_category set to 'not_applicable' and glycemic_index set to 0 as a numeric sentinel indicating no glycaemic response.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050mg/100g — far exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold; potassium at 47mg/100g provides negligible offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit grilled pork skewers due to the very high sodium content of 1050mg/100g, which is driven by soy sauce and oyster sauce marinades; if consumed, portion should be limited to one skewer and paired with high-potassium foods such as sweet potato or leafy greens to partially offset sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content; high protein (19.1g/100g) supports satiety without glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled pork skewers are suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as the carbohydrate and sugar content are both 0g/100g, producing no glycaemic load; however, marinade may contain hidden sugars and the high sodium content warrants caution in those with concurrent hypertension, so requesting reduced-sugar and reduced-sodium preparation is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1050mg/100g elevates blood pressure risk; cholesterol 89mg/100g and unquantified saturated fat from pork contribute to atherogenic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume grilled pork skewers only occasionally and in small portions, as the very high sodium content (1050mg/100g) directly raises blood pressure, and the cholesterol content (89mg/100g) combined with the saturated fat inherent in pork contributes to LDL elevation and atherosclerotic risk; grilling reduces added fat compared to frying but does not eliminate the intrinsic lipid and sodium burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; high sodium exacerbates joint swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit consumption of grilled pork skewers, as pork is a primary dietary source of arachidonic acid that drives pro-inflammatory eicosanoid production, and the high sodium content of 1050mg/100g further exacerbates synovial fluid retention and joint swelling; grilling at high heat also generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) which may worsen systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Pork Skewers (烤猪肉串, Kǎo zhū ròu chuàn) are a popular Chinese-style street food and hawker dish widely available in Singapore. The dish consists of seasoned pork pieces threaded onto bamboo or metal skewers and cooked over direct high heat, typically charcoal or gas grill. The marinade commonly includes soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, five-spice powder, and sometimes sugar, imparting a savoury, slightly caramelised flavour with a lightly charred exterior and juicy interior. A standard serving is three skewers (approximately 90g). Nutritionally, the dish is high in protein (19.1g/100g) and moderate in fat (10.5g/100g), with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar per 100g, making it glycaemically inert. However, the sodium content is very high at 1050mg/100g, primarily from the soy and oyster sauce marinade, which is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Cholesterol is 89mg/100g. The dish is not suitable for halal diets as it contains pork. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner item, often paired with rice or noodles in hawker centre settings across Singapore.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in grilled pork skewers?
Is 烤猪肉串 high in calories?
What is the protein content of pork skewers?
Is grilled pork suitable for hypertension?
How many calories in 3 pork skewers?
Is pork satay the same as grilled pork skewers?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact dish (cr_id G00010920, 烤猪肉串 Grilled Pork, Three Strings 3串) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Source match type is exact. No estimation or analogy was required for core macros.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — this is a protein-and-fat dominant grilled meat dish with no starch, bread, or sugar-based components in the base preparation; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo; marinade may contain trace sugar in practice but the HPB canonical value reports 0g/100g. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a pure meat dish with no plant fibre components per HPB Singapore Foodo. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g/100g); gi_category set to 'not_applicable' and glycemic_index set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value indicating no glycaemic response is expected. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this grilled pork skewer dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data beyond those reported by HPB Singapore Foodo are not available in the HPB Foodo database for this specific dish entry as of 2026. The dish is not halal-certified as it contains pork. Sodium is extremely high at 1050mg/100g — primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management.
